project.name: mini-claude
project.description: A miniature AI coding assistant CLI that uses LangGraph state machine for multi-step task execution with parallel sub-agent support

nodes.name: Task Execution
nodes.summary: Core domain responsible for processing user requests through a THINK-PLAN-ACT-OBSERVE state machine loop. Handles single-step and multi-step tasks with tool orchestration.

businessRules: Maximum 10 iterations for main agent, 5 for sub-agents
Idle loop detection stops after 3 consecutive iterations without tool calls
Read-only tool loop detection stops after 2 consecutive read-only operations
crossDomainInteractions: Invokes Tool System for file/command operations
Spawns Sub-Agent Management for parallel execution
Uses LLM Integration for reasoning and response generation

nodes.name: Tool System
nodes.summary: Registry and execution layer for all available tools. Provides file operations, command execution, web search, and agent management tools with safety checks and file locking.

businessRules: All tools must extend BaseTool and implement execute()
File operations require safety checks before execution
Write operations acquire file locks to prevent conflicts
crossDomainInteractions: Called by Task Execution domain during ACT phase
Used by Sub-Agent Management for parallel file operations

nodes.name: Sub-Agent Management
nodes.summary: Manages parallel execution of sub-agents with semaphore-based concurrency control. Coordinates task distribution, result aggregation, and conflict detection.

businessRules: Sub-agents cannot spawn more agents (prevents infinite recursion)
Dependency analysis determines execution order with topological sort
crossDomainInteractions: Invokes Task Execution domain logic within each sub-agent
Uses Tool System for file locking and conflict detection
Returns aggregated results to main agent

nodes.name: LLM Integration
nodes.summary: Multi-provider LLM abstraction layer using LiteLLM. Handles message formatting, tool calling, streaming output, and provider-specific prompt customization.

businessRules: Supports Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, DeepSeek, Ollama providers
Each provider has customized system prompt format
Streaming disabled for sub-agents to simplify result collection
crossDomainInteractions: Called by Task Execution during THINK and ACT phases
Tool definitions converted to LiteLLM format
Messages converted from LangChain format to LiteLLM format

nodes.name: User Interaction
nodes.summary: REPL-based command-line interface with history, auto-suggest, and session management. Handles slash commands, streaming display, and multi-thread conversations.

businessRules: Conversation history limited to last 20 messages
Thread switching preserves separate conversation states
crossDomainInteractions: Triggers Task Execution domain with user input

nodes.name: Process User Request
nodes.summary: Main request processing flow through THINK-PLAN-ACT-OBSERVE state machine loop.

nodes.name: Receive Input
nodes.summary: Accept user input from REPL or API, handle slash commands if present.

nodes.name: Think
nodes.summary: Analyze the request, check iteration limits, inject system prompt, prepare for planning.

nodes.name: Plan
nodes.summary: Create execution plan based on task keywords, detect required tools, check for multi-file project completion.

nodes.name: Act
nodes.summary: Execute tools via LLM tool calling, handle streaming output, process tool results immediately.

nodes.name: Observe
nodes.summary: Process tool results, detect completion conditions, check for idle loops, decide whether to continue.

nodes.name: Respond
nodes.summary: Extract final response from messages, display to user, update conversation history.

nodes.name: Execute File Operation
nodes.summary: Handle file read/write/edit operations with safety checks and file locking.

nodes.name: Validate Request
nodes.summary: Check path safety, resolve relative paths to workspace, validate parameters.

nodes.name: Acquire Lock
nodes.summary: Acquire read or write lock on target file, check for conflicts with other agents.

nodes.name: Execute Operation
nodes.summary: Perform the actual file read/write/edit operation, create parent directories if needed.

nodes.name: Release Lock
nodes.summary: Release file lock, update version tracking for write operations.

nodes.name: Spawn Parallel Agents
nodes.summary: Create and execute multiple sub-agents in parallel for independent tasks.

nodes.name: Analyze Dependencies
nodes.summary: Build dependency graph, perform topological sort, identify execution levels.

nodes.name: Detect Conflicts
nodes.summary: Identify tasks that modify the same files, warn about potential conflicts.

nodes.name: Launch Agents
nodes.summary: Create asyncio tasks for each agent, use semaphore to limit concurrency.

nodes.name: Wait for Completion
nodes.summary: Use asyncio.gather to wait for all agents, handle timeouts and exceptions.

nodes.name: Aggregate Results
nodes.summary: Collect results from all completed agents, generate summary report.

nodes.name: Sub-Agent Execution
nodes.summary: Execute a task within a sub-agent context with limited tools and iterations.

nodes.name: Create State
nodes.summary: Initialize sub-agent state with task description, mark is_subagent=True, set allowed_tools.

nodes.name: Run Graph
nodes.summary: Execute the same THINK-PLAN-ACT-OBSERVE graph with sub-agent constraints.

nodes.name: Extract Result
nodes.summary: Find the last AI message without tool calls as the final result.

nodes.name: Auto Fallback
nodes.summary: If LLM fails to create file, auto-create with basic content based on task description.

nodes.name: Manage Session
nodes.summary: Handle conversation session persistence, thread switching, and history management.

nodes.name: Save Session
nodes.summary: Serialize conversation messages to SQLite database with thread ID.

nodes.name: Load Session
nodes.summary: Retrieve conversation history from database by thread ID.

nodes.name: Switch Thread
nodes.summary: Change active thread ID and clear current conversation buffer.

edges.description: Task Execution invokes tools during ACT phase to perform file/command operations
Task Execution spawns sub-agents for parallel task execution via spawn_parallel tool
Task Execution calls LLM for reasoning (THINK), planning (PLAN), and tool selection (ACT)
Task Execution receives input from User Interaction and returns responses
Sub-agents use Tool System for file locking and conflict detection during parallel writes
Sub-agents call LLM independently with sub-agent specific prompts and constraints
User Interaction displays streaming output from LLM Integration
